testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Server function: getShipPrep (preflight data)
acceptanceCriteria: Resolves project path server-side from `ticketId`/`epicId`
Returns all preflight fields correctly
`changedFiles` includes tracked, untracked, deleted, and renamed files
`isSafeToShip` is false for main/master/develop branches
`reviewMarkerFresh` uses a 5-minute window from file mtime
All subprocess calls use shared no-shell helpers
Parser behavior is covered by tests for realistic `git status --short` output
`pnpm type-check` passes
userStories.description:
Create `src/api/ship-server-fns.ts` with the `getShipPrep` server function that gathers all preflight data needed before the Ship Changes modal can show its file picker and checks.

## Function signature
```ts
getShipPrep({ ticketId?, epicId? })
  → {
      changedFiles: { path: string; status: string }[];
      currentBranch: string;
      isSafeToShip: boolean;
      reviewMarkerFresh: boolean;
      ghAvailable: boolean;
      remoteConfigured: boolean;
      inferredScope: { type: 'ticket' | 'epic'; id: string; title: string } | null;
    }
```

## Implementation notes
- Resolve `projectPath` on the server from trusted DB records using `ticketId` or `epicId` — never trust a raw client path for git/gh operations
- All subprocess calls via the shared `execFileNoThrow` helper using array args only
- Use shared parser helpers where possible so later ship functions reuse the same git-status parsing behavior
- `git status --short` → parse each line into `{ path, status }`; include untracked (`??`) and modified/deleted/renamed files
- `git branch --show-current` → `currentBranch`
- `isSafeToShip`: false when branch is in `['main', 'master', 'develop']`
- `reviewMarkerFresh`: check `.claude/.review-completed` mtime < 5 minutes
- `ghAvailable`: check `which gh`
- `remoteConfigured`: `git remote` returns at least one remote
- `inferredScope`: if `ticketId`/`epicId` passed, fetch title from DB; else null
- Server function should follow the same POST wrapper pattern used by `workflow-server-fns.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resolves project path server-side from `ticketId`/`epicId`
- [ ] Returns all preflight fields correctly
- [ ] `changedFiles` includes tracked, untracked, deleted, and renamed files
- [ ] `isSafeToShip` is false for main/master/develop branches
- [ ] `reviewMarkerFresh` uses a 5-minute window from file mtime
- [ ] All subprocess calls use shared no-shell helpers
- [ ] Parser behavior is covered by tests for realistic `git status --short` output
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

acceptanceCriteria: Stages only `selectedPaths` (never `git add -A`)
Resolves project path server-side from trusted scope IDs
Parses commit hash correctly from real git output
Epic scope links commit metadata to every ticket assigned to the epic
Epic scope links PR metadata to every ticket assigned to the epic
Epic scope updates `epic_workflow_state` so the epic detail header reflects the PR immediately
PR body includes acceptance criteria as checkboxes
PR body includes `<!-- brain-dump:demo-steps -->` sentinel
All subprocess calls use shared no-shell helpers
Returns structured errors instead of throwing to the caller
Includes tests for commit hash parsing, PR URL/number parsing, and epic-wide linkage persistence
`pnpm type-check` passes
userStories.description:
Add `commitAndShipServerFn` to `src/api/ship-server-fns.ts` — the core function that stages selected files, commits, pushes, creates a PR, and persists linkage for both ticket and epic scopes. This ticket also owns the PR body generator utility.

## Function signature
```ts
commitAndShipServerFn({
  scopeType: 'ticket' | 'epic',
  scopeId: string,
  message: string,
  selectedPaths: string[],
  prTitle: string,
  prBody: string,
  draft: boolean
}) → { success, commitHash?, prNumber?, prUrl?, error? }
```

## Steps (server-side, in order)
1. Resolve trusted scope context from DB (`projectPath`, branch, ticket/epic metadata)
2. `git add -- <selectedPaths>`
3. `git commit -m <message>` → parse commit hash from stdout
4. Link commit metadata
   - ticket scope: link to that ticket
   - epic scope: link to every ticket currently assigned to the epic so the audit trail matches the shared epic PR strategy
5. `git push -u origin <currentBranch>`
6. `gh pr create --title <prTitle> --body <prBody> [--draft]`
7. Parse PR number and URL from `gh` stdout
8. Persist PR linkage
   - ticket scope: `linkPr` for that ticket
   - epic scope: `linkPr` for every ticket currently assigned to the epic
9. If epic scope, update `epic_workflow_state` with `pr_number`, `pr_url`, `pr_status`, and `updated_at`
10. Return `{ success: true, commitHash, prNumber, prUrl }`

## PR body generator
Expose `generatePrBodyServerFn({ scopeType, scopeId })` that returns the generated body string used to pre-populate the modal before shipping.

### Requirements
- Epic scope must include the epic title/description, all tickets in the epic, acceptance criteria checkboxes from ticket subtasks/criteria, the demo-step sentinel block, and implementation notes from relevant work-summary comments
- Ticket scope uses the same structure for a single ticket
- Output should make the linked PR clearly attributable to the epic or ticket that created it

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stages only `selectedPaths` (never `git add -A`)
- [ ] Resolves project path server-side from trusted scope IDs
- [ ] Parses commit hash correctly from real git output
- [ ] Epic scope links commit metadata to every ticket assigned to the epic
- [ ] Epic scope links PR metadata to every ticket assigned to the epic
- [ ] Epic scope updates `epic_workflow_state` so the epic detail header reflects the PR immediately
- [ ] PR body includes acceptance criteria as checkboxes
- [ ] PR body includes `<!-- brain-dump:demo-steps -->` sentinel
- [ ] All subprocess calls use shared no-shell helpers
- [ ] Returns structured errors instead of throwing to the caller
- [ ] Includes tests for commit hash parsing, PR URL/number parsing, and epic-wide linkage persistence
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

acceptanceCriteria: Reads demo steps from the real `demo_scripts.steps` JSON storage model
Updates the PR body sentinel block with rendered demo steps
Is idempotent: running twice with the same steps does not duplicate content
Returns early cleanly if no PR is linked to the ticket
`review.generate-demo` invokes sync after saving steps
Sync failure logs a warning but does not break demo generation
All gh calls use shared no-shell helpers
Includes tests for sentinel replacement and idempotent behavior
`pnpm type-check` passes
userStories.description:
Add `syncPrVerificationChecklistServerFn` to `src/api/ship-server-fns.ts` and wire shared sync logic into `mcp-server/tools/review.ts` after `generate-demo` completes.

## Server function
```ts
syncPrVerificationChecklistServerFn({ ticketId })
  → { success, prUrl?, error? }
```

## Steps
1. Fetch ticket from DB and read `prNumber`/`prUrl`; if no PR is linked, return early with success
2. Fetch the latest demo script row for the ticket from `demo_scripts`
3. Parse the real `steps` JSON payload from `demo_scripts.steps`
4. Fetch the current PR body via `gh pr view <prNumber> --json body --jq .body`
5. Find content beginning at `<!-- brain-dump:demo-steps -->` and replace that block up to the next `##` header or EOF
6. Render the demo steps in a stable numbered markdown format
7. `gh pr edit <prNumber> --body <updatedBody>`
8. Return `{ success: true, prUrl }`

## Shared-core requirement
The MCP server is standalone and cannot call TanStack Start server functions directly. Extract the core sync/render logic into a shared helper that both the server fn and `mcp-server/tools/review.ts` can import.

## `review.generate-demo` hook
After demo steps are saved in `mcp-server/tools/review.ts`:
- Check whether the ticket already has a linked PR
- If yes, call the shared sync helper
- If sync fails, log a warning and continue; demo generation must still succeed
- Add sync outcome details to the MCP response text so the agent sees whether the PR body was updated

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reads demo steps from the real `demo_scripts.steps` JSON storage model
- [ ] Updates the PR body sentinel block with rendered demo steps
- [ ] Is idempotent: running twice with the same steps does not duplicate content
- [ ] Returns early cleanly if no PR is linked to the ticket
- [ ] `review.generate-demo` invokes sync after saving steps
- [ ] Sync failure logs a warning but does not break demo generation
- [ ] All gh calls use shared no-shell helpers
- [ ] Includes tests for sentinel replacement and idempotent behavior
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

userStories.title: UI: ShipChangesModal component with all 5 states
acceptanceCriteria: Modal opens and immediately fetches preflight data and generated PR body
File picker shows tracked + untracked files with status prefixes
Select All works correctly
Preflight checks render green/red/yellow states clearly
blocked-review appears when review marker is stale and Recheck works
blocked-main appears on protected branches and branch creation returns to preflight
running state shows step progression and prevents dismissal
done state shows commit hash and PR URL with copy/open actions
error state allows retry and keeps failures visible to the user
Keyboard behavior works: Escape closes unless running; Enter submits when valid
No silent failures: all errors are surfaced in the UI
Component tests cover at least one happy path and key blocked/error states
`pnpm type-check && pnpm lint` pass
userStories.description:
Create `src/components/tickets/ShipChangesModal.tsx` — the unified Ship Changes modal with 5 explicit states.

## Props
```ts
{
  isOpen: boolean;
  onClose: () => void;
  projectPath: string; // UI only: used for terminal-launch convenience
  scopeType: 'ticket' | 'epic';
  scopeId: string;
  scopeTitle: string;
  branchName?: string;
  onSuccess: (prUrl: string) => void;
}
```

## Important boundary
- `projectPath` may be passed to support "Run Review" / terminal-launch UX
- Ship mutations themselves (`getShipPrep`, `commitAndShipServerFn`, `pushBranchServerFn`) must resolve trusted project context server-side from IDs, not from this prop

## State machine
```text
preflight → [blocked-review | blocked-main] → running → done | error
```

On open, immediately load preflight data and the generated PR body.

## State UIs
### preflight
- Preflight panel with branch, review freshness, GitHub CLI, and remote checks
- File picker showing `<status> <path>` rows with checkboxes and a Select All toggle
- Editable commit message prefilled `feat(<shortId>): <scopeTitle>`
- Editable PR title
- Collapsible editable PR body pre-populated from `generatePrBodyServerFn`
- Draft PR checkbox (default checked)
- CTA disabled when gh/remote checks fail or no files are selected

### blocked-review
- Warn that review must be run before shipping
- "Run Review" launches a terminal in the project dir using existing terminal-launch logic
- "Recheck" re-runs `getShipPrep`

### blocked-main
- Warn that direct pushes to main/master/develop are not allowed
- "Create feature branch and continue" uses existing workflow start helpers, then re-runs preflight

### running
- Non-dismissible progress UI with steps for staging, committing, pushing, and creating the PR

### done
- Success UI showing commit hash, PR URL, copy/open actions, and close behavior via `onSuccess`

### error
- Inline error state that shows the failing step and allows retry back to preflight

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Modal opens and immediately fetches preflight data and generated PR body
- [ ] File picker shows tracked + untracked files with status prefixes
- [ ] Select All works correctly
- [ ] Preflight checks render green/red/yellow states clearly
- [ ] blocked-review appears when review marker is stale and Recheck works
- [ ] blocked-main appears on protected branches and branch creation returns to preflight
- [ ] running state shows step progression and prevents dismissal
- [ ] done state shows commit hash and PR URL with copy/open actions
- [ ] error state allows retry and keeps failures visible to the user
- [ ] Keyboard behavior works: Escape closes unless running; Enter submits when valid
- [ ] No silent failures: all errors are surfaced in the UI
- [ ] Component tests cover at least one happy path and key blocked/error states
- [ ] `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint` pass

Ticket detail header shows `Push` when the ticket already has a PR

userStories.description:
Wire the `ShipChangesModal` into both entry points and add push-only buttons for subsequent pushes when a PR already exists.

## Epic detail page (`src/routes/epic.$id.tsx`)
This is the primary entry point.

### Ship Changes button
- Visible when the epic has a workflow branch
- Opens `ShipChangesModal` with `scopeType="epic"`
- Uses `project.path` already returned by epic detail data for terminal-launch UX only
- On success: invalidate the epic detail query so PR badge/status refresh immediately

### Push button
- Visible when the epic already has a linked PR in `epic_workflow_state`
- Inline action with spinner/toast feedback
- Calls `pushBranchServerFn({ scopeType: 'epic', scopeId: epic.id })`

## Ticket detail header (`src/components/tickets/TicketDetailHeader.tsx`)
This is the secondary entry point.

### Ship button
- Visible when `ticket.branchName` exists
- Positioned before Edit: `[Ship] [Edit] [Start Work]`
- Add `onShip: () => void` to `TicketDetailHeaderProps`

### Push button
- Visible when `ticket.prNumber` exists
- Inline action with spinner/toast feedback
- Calls `pushBranchServerFn({ scopeType: 'ticket', scopeId: ticket.id })`

## `ticket.$id.tsx` changes
- Add `showShipModal` state
- Reuse existing project data access rather than inventing a new `getProject` API; derive the project from existing hooks/query data and pass `project.path` only for terminal UX
- Render `<ShipChangesModal>` when open
- On modal success: invalidate the ticket query so PR badge/status refresh immediately

## `pushBranchServerFn`
Add to `src/api/ship-server-fns.ts`:
```ts
pushBranchServerFn({ scopeType: 'ticket' | 'epic', scopeId: string })
  → { success, error? }
```

### Requirements
- Resolve trusted `projectPath` + branch server-side from DB/workflow state
- `git push origin <branch>` via shared no-shell helper
- Return structured errors for toast display

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Epic detail page shows `Ship Changes` when the epic has a branch
- [ ] Epic detail page shows `Push` when the epic already has a PR
- [ ] Ticket detail header shows `Ship` when the ticket has a branch
- [ ] Ticket detail header shows `Push` when the ticket already has a PR
- [ ] Modal opens from both entry points with the correct scope
- [ ] Push-only action shows loading state and success/error toast feedback
- [ ] Ticket detail invalidates/refetches on successful ship
- [ ] Epic detail invalidates/refetches on successful ship
- [ ] Manual QA from the epic detail page covers the full ship flow end-to-end
- [ ] `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint` pass

userStories.description:
Add the shared groundwork required by the Ship Changes epic so the later server and MCP tickets build on one secure implementation instead of duplicating parsing and subprocess logic.

## Scope
1. Introduce the shared `execFileNoThrow`-style helper used by ship-related git/gh calls
2. Add shared helpers for:
   - resolving trusted ship scope (`ticket` or `epic`) to project path / branch / metadata
   - parsing `git status --short` output
   - parsing commit hashes from `git commit` output
   - parsing PR number/URL from `gh pr create` output
   - replacing the `<!-- brain-dump:demo-steps -->` sentinel block idempotently
3. Put shared non-UI logic in a location that can be imported by both app server functions and the standalone MCP server
4. If needed, keep a thin app-side wrapper at the documented path so repo guidance stays accurate

## Why this exists
- The current ship plan assumes `execFileNoThrow`, but that helper does not exist yet
- Review sync needs shared logic across TanStack server functions and `mcp-server/tools/review.ts`
- Epic/ticket shipping should not each invent their own git parsing rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shared no-shell process helper exists and is reusable from ship-related code
- [ ] Shared parser helpers exist for git status, commit output, PR output, and sentinel replacement
- [ ] Shared scope-resolution helper resolves trusted project/branch context from DB IDs
- [ ] Helper locations support both app server functions and MCP usage
- [ ] Tests cover parser helpers with realistic command output fixtures
- [ ] Any repo documentation that points to the helper path is updated if necessary
- [ ] `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint` pass

userStories.description:
Add user-centered test coverage for the full Ship Changes experience after implementation is complete. Follow a Kent C. Dodds style: test what the user sees, can click, and what feedback they receive.

## Scope
### Component/integration coverage
- Epic detail page shows the correct `Ship Changes` / `Push` actions for the current state
- Ticket detail header shows the correct `Ship` / `Push` actions for the current state
- ShipChangesModal renders and transitions through preflight, blocked-review, blocked-main, running, done, and error states
- File picker selection, Select All, disabled CTA states, and keyboard behavior work as expected
- Success path shows commit hash, PR link, and success feedback
- Failure paths surface visible feedback for:
  - missing `gh`
  - missing remote
  - protected branch
  - stale/missing review marker
  - no files selected
  - commit failure
  - push failure
  - PR creation failure
  - push-only failure
- `review.generate-demo` PR-body sync success/failure is covered at the shared-helper level and does not silently fail

### Manual QA checklist
Create a concise visual/behavioral test matrix covering:
- epic ship happy path
- ticket ship happy path
- push-only happy path
- blocked-review recovery path
- blocked-main recovery path
- server error path with visible UI feedback
- post-ship refresh showing PR badges/status on epic and ticket views

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Automated tests focus on user-visible behavior instead of implementation details
- [ ] Happy-path tests exist for both epic and ticket entry points
- [ ] Failure-path tests exist for the major blocked/error states
- [ ] Query invalidation/refresh is verified through visible post-action UI state
- [ ] Manual QA checklist is added for final verification
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes
- [ ] `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint` pass

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicTitle: Ship Changes: UI Commit, Push & PR Flow
epicDescription:
Close the gap in the dev workflow: developers currently must run `git commit`, `git push`, and `gh pr create` manually after ticket work. This epic adds a unified "Ship Changes" flow directly in the Brain Dump UI.

## Primary entry point: Epic detail page
The main use case is shipping an entire epic's worth of work as one PR from the epic detail page.

## Secondary entry point: Ticket detail header
Individual ticket branches can also be shipped from the ticket detail page.

## What gets built
1. Foundation helpers for secure process execution and shared ship/parsing logic
2. Server functions for preflight checks, commit+push+PR, push-only, and PR body sync
3. A unified ShipChangesModal with 5 states (preflight, blocked-review, blocked-main, running, done)
4. Entry point buttons on the epic detail page and ticket detail header
5. Auto-sync of PR body when demo steps are generated
6. User-facing success/failure test coverage for the whole flow

## Key success conditions
- Epic PR creation must update `epic_workflow_state` and link PR metadata to every ticket currently assigned to that epic so the epic header and ticket headers stay in sync
- Server-side git/gh actions must resolve `projectPath` from trusted DB records using `ticketId`/`epicId`; UI-provided paths are only for terminal-launch UX
- PR body sync must read demo steps from the real `demo_scripts.steps` JSON payload and update the sentinel block idempotently
- All subprocess calls use array args with a shared no-throw helper (no shell interpolation)
- Testing follows a Kent C. Dodds style: validate what the user sees and can do, with explicit success and failure cases

## Key design decisions
- File selection picker (not blind `git add -A`) — user sees and picks which files to commit
- Preflight checks: branch safety, review marker freshness, gh available, remote configured
- PR body auto-populated with acceptance criteria checkboxes and demo steps from Brain Dump
- `<!-- brain-dump:demo-steps -->` sentinel in PR body auto-updates when demo steps are generated
- All subprocess calls use execFileNoThrow-style helpers (no shell interpolation)

## Revised execution order
1. Foundation: add shared process/ship helpers and parser coverage
2. Server: getShipPrep
3. Server: commitAndShip + PR body generation + epic-wide PR linkage
4. Server/MCP: PR verification checklist sync after `review.generate-demo`
5. UI: ShipChangesModal
6. UI: entry points and push-only actions
7. Tests: user-facing success/failure coverage and visual QA checklist
